title: Tartar Sauce Glazed Salmon with Citrusy Salsa
description: A refreshing seafood dish featuring a citrusy twist on a classic tartar sauce and a tangy salsa to complement the flavors.

ingredients.name: Salmon fillets (6 oz each)
ingredients.notes: Fresh salmon for a flaky texture

ingredients.name: Lime juice
ingredients.notes: To add a zesty twist to the tartar sauce

ingredients.name: Orange juice
ingredients.notes: To give the salmon a citrusy glaze

ingredients.name: Chewy dates
ingredients.notes: To add natural sweetness and chew

ingredients.name: Chopped cilantro
ingredients.notes: For a fresh and herbaceous flavor

ingredients.name: Red onion
ingredients.notes: For a sweet and crunchy texture

ingredients.name: Lime zest
ingredients.notes: For a burst of citrus flavor

ingredients.name: Pepper
ingredients.notes: To add a touch of spice

instructions: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Place the salmon fillets on the prepared baking sheet.
In a small bowl, whisk together the tartar sauce, lime juice, and honey until smooth.
Brush the tartar sauce mixture evenly over the salmon fillets.
Bake for 12-15 minutes or until the salmon is cooked through.
Meanwhile, prepare the citrusy salsa by combining the chopped cilantro, red onion, lime zest, and chopped dates in a bowl.
Squeeze the lime juice over the salsa and toss to combine.
To serve, place the salmon fillets on a plate and top with a spoonful of the citrusy salsa.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
